plainEnglish: Live audio in video—webinars, live streams, broadcasts—needs real-time captions as it happens. People who are deaf or hard of hearing need to follow live content too, so caption it using a live captioning service (CART) or your streaming platform's real-time caption feature.
whyItMatters: Deaf and hard-of-hearing viewers can't follow a live webinar or stream that has no real-time captions.
quickCheck: Join your own live stream as a viewer and confirm captions appear in near-real-time; there's no automated check for live captioning.
commonMistakes: Planning to publish captions only after the event instead of captioning it live.
Relying solely on a platform's automatic captions, with no human captioner, for technical or heavily-named content.
Forgetting to turn on the streaming platform's caption feature before going live.

codeExamples.label: Live player wired to a real-time caption track
codeExamples.code:
<video controls src="/live-stream.m3u8">
  <track
    kind="captions"
    src="/live-captions.vtt"
    srcLang="en"
    label="Live captions"
    default
  />
</video>
